text:
BOTTOM LINE — Mixed tape, defensive sectors won. Equities drifted lower on low conviction as QQQ hit a distribution signal and oil surged 2.20% pressuring discretionary; the rotation into utilities and materials revealed a cautious hedge against the AI hardware unwind.

INDICES — VIX 16.6, flat below its 20d MA. SPY -0.12%, DIA -0.01%, IWM -0.93%, QQQ -0.51%, ^SOX +0.51%. Large > small by 0.42pp. Gold +1.15%.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.9x, QQQ 0.7x — QQQ showing distribution (volume down, price down = low-conviction sell). Elevated volume in GLD 1.1x (accumulation) and XLU 1.1x (accumulation), confirming defensive money-in. Ignored: SOXX 0.7x, XLK 0.6x.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLU Utilities +2.25%, XLB Materials +1.44%, XLE Energy +1.20%, XLP Staples +0.38%, XLI Industrials +0.11%, XLF Financials -0.11%, XLK Technology -0.28%, XLRE Real Estate -0.42%, XLV Healthcare -0.51%, XLY Discretionary -0.74%, XLC Comms -0.75%. Top Industries: GDX +3.36%, UTES +2.72%. Bottom: FDN -1.86%, IGV -3.05%. Money rotating out of tech and discretionary into utilities and materials. Rotation state: fading AI hardware / high-to-low rotation.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.40 elevated, QQQ PCR_vol 1.53 elevated — heavy put protection demand. SPY IV skew +10.4% fear; price 747.2 below gamma flip 756.0 — dealer hedging amplifies downside. VIX at 16.6 below its 20d MA 16.8, showing suppressed realized fear despite options premiums.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +2.20% diverging from equities, with XLY Discretionary -0.74% and airlines (DRAM -1.84%) selling off on energy cost fears. Gold +1.15% rising alongside dollar weakness — unusual safe-haven bid.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63.6 Greed. Top movers: GEV +3.36% (Industrials), NOW -6.47% (Software, vol spike 2.3x — distribution), GDX +3.36% (Gold Miners). Best 20d momentum: XLE Energy +9.51%; worst: SOXX -15.19%. Worst basket: IGV -3.05% (Software). Watch: NOW (-6.47%, vol spike distribution heading into earnings), CB (-3.26%, insurance vol 2.6x distribution), GEV (+3.36%, accumulation divergence from weakening industrials).
